pr.title: Tweak `Makefile` to be more convenient with `objdiff`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Added an objdiff-focused make wrapper and a Makefile WIP mode. The new `tools/objdiff-make.sh` runs `make` with map generation, one-error limit, parallel jobs, `WINE=wibo`, and `WIP=1`; the Makefile uses `WIP=1` to skip checks, disable required prototypes, isolate outputs under `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2`, and define `-DWIP` during compilation. This made objdiff iteration more convenient without disturbing the normal build directory.
what_changed: When `WIP=1`, the Makefile now sets `SKIP_CHECK := 1` and `REQUIRE_PROTOS := 0`.
When `WIP=1`, `BUILD_DIR` changes from `build/$(TARGET)` to `build/wip/$(TARGET)`, isolating objdiff or work-in-progress outputs from the normal build.
Updated the `clean` target to remove `$(BUILD_DIR)` instead of hard-coding `build/ssbm.us.1.2`.
Added executable script `tools/objdiff-make.sh` that invokes `make GENERATE_MAP=1 MAX_ERRORS=1 WIP=1 "-j$(nproc)" WINE=wibo "$@"`.
PR body instructs users to set objdiff's Custom make program to `tools/objdiff-make.sh` and notes it requires `wibo` on the path, with a Windows workaround suggestion.
smart_moves: Kept normal builds unchanged by making WIP behavior opt-in through `WIP=1`.
Separated WIP artifacts into `build/wip/$(TARGET)`, reducing the chance that objdiff experiments contaminate or invalidate the regular build outputs.
Bundled common objdiff settings into a small wrapper script so users do not need to repeatedly configure `GENERATE_MAP=1`, `MAX_ERRORS=1`, `WIP=1`, parallel jobs, and `WINE=wibo` manually.
Limited compiler errors to one in the objdiff workflow via `MAX_ERRORS=1`, which is useful for fast feedback during iterative matching work.
Made the clean target respect the selected build directory by switching from a hard-coded target build path to `$(BUILD_DIR)`.
decomp_lessons: For decomp workflows, add opt-in build modes for incomplete work rather than weakening the default verification path.
Objdiff integration benefits from a dedicated make entrypoint that standardizes flags and environment assumptions.
Use a separate WIP build directory when allowing skipped checks or missing prototypes, so experimental object files do not mix with canonical build artifacts.
If a tool workflow requires alternate Wine handling, make that explicit in the wrapper and document the expected binary.
naming_conventions: The opt-in work-in-progress build mode is named `WIP` and is enabled with `WIP=1`.
The WIP output directory convention introduced here is `build/wip/$(TARGET)`.
The objdiff custom make wrapper is named `tools/objdiff-make.sh`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use objdiff's Custom make program setting to point at `tools/objdiff-make.sh` for faster matching iterations.
The wrapper builds with `GENERATE_MAP=1`, which supports symbol/map inspection during object comparison.
The wrapper builds with `MAX_ERRORS=1` and parallel `-j$(nproc)` to speed up compile-test cycles.
The WIP mode disables prototype requirements and skips checks, allowing partially decompiled or experimental code to compile for objdiff comparison.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that use or modify `WIP=1` in the Makefile.
Find later changes to `tools/objdiff-make.sh` or objdiff workflow documentation.
Check whether `-DWIP` became used in source files and what conditional code it gated.
Look for later build-system changes involving `build/wip` or separate experimental output directories.
